---
title: "Updating genomes and indices"
author: "AY"
---

This page contains the instruction for `./scripts/bcfngs genomes`.
It covers:

- checking whether the pinned genome release is outdated (`check-updates`)
- planning and building genome sources plus indices (`plan`)
- rebuilding only selected organisms (`build`)
- updating genome configuration and versioned index directories
- advanced direct `snakemake` usage when the wrapper does not expose a knob

Environment setup is documented elsewhere. This page assumes `./scripts/bcfngs` already runs on your machine or cluster.

## Command overview

Before running any genome subcommand, you can list the configured organisms directly from the wrapper:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs --list-org
```

This prints the organism key, assembly/build label, source, and pinned release defined in [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml).

`bcfngs genomes` currently exposes three subcommands:

- `check-updates`: compare the pinned Ensembl release in [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) with the latest Ensembl release visible on `ftp.ensembl.org`
- `plan`: run preflight checks, `snakemake --lint`, and a dry-run
- `build`: xecute the workflow for real; unless stated otherwise (`-no-skip-plan`), thus will try to build the indices without first running `plan`.

Inspect the wrapper help first. Current output from the script:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes --help
```

```text
usage: bcfngs genomes [-h] {check-updates,plan,build} ...

positional arguments:
  {check-updates,plan,build}

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
```

### Planning genome indexing

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan --help
```

```text
usage: bcfngs genomes plan [-h] [--organism ORGANISM]
                           [--star-index-version STAR_INDEX_VERSION]
                           [--bwa-index-version BWA_INDEX_VERSION]
                           [--bowtie2-index-version BOWTIE2_INDEX_VERSION]

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --organism ORGANISM   Comma-separated subset of organism keys (e.g.
                        Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus)
  --star-index-version STAR_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this STAR index version label.
  --bwa-index-version BWA_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this bwa index version label.
  --bowtie2-index-version BOWTIE2_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this bowtie2 index version label.
```

### Building the genomic indicies

This step can be either done locally or using the slurm scheduler of our HPC.

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --help
```

```text
usage: bcfngs genomes build [-h] [--executor {local,slurm}] [--cores CORES] [--jobs JOBS] [--slurm-partition SLURM_PARTITION]
                            [--slurm-account SLURM_ACCOUNT] [--slurm-qos SLURM_QOS] [--slurm-mail-user SLURM_MAIL_USER]
                            [--slurm-mail-type SLURM_MAIL_TYPE] [--slurm-time SLURM_TIME] [--slurm-cpus-per-task SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK]
                            [--slurm-mem-per-cpu SLURM_MEM_PER_CPU] [--slurm-mem SLURM_MEM] [--slurm-exclusive] [--organism ORGANISM]
                            [--star-index-version STAR_INDEX_VERSION] [--bwa-index-version BWA_INDEX_VERSION]
                            [--bowtie2-index-version BOWTIE2_INDEX_VERSION] [-no-skip-plan]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --executor {local,slurm}
                        Execution backend: local (default) or slurm.
  --cores CORES
  --jobs JOBS           Max concurrent jobs for --executor slurm.
  --slurm-partition SLURM_PARTITION
                        SLURM partition name (for --executor slurm).
  --slurm-account SLURM_ACCOUNT
                        SLURM account name (for --executor slurm).
  --slurm-qos SLURM_QOS
                        SLURM QoS name (for --executor slurm).
  --slurm-mail-user SLURM_MAIL_USER
                        SLURM mail user for --executor slurm.
  --slurm-mail-type SLURM_MAIL_TYPE
                        SLURM mail type for --executor slurm (e.g. ALL or END,FAIL).
  --slurm-time SLURM_TIME
                        SLURM walltime HH:MM:SS (default: 04:00:00).
  --slurm-cpus-per-task SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK
                        SLURM cpus-per-task for heavy index rules (default: 20).
  --slurm-mem-per-cpu SLURM_MEM_PER_CPU
                        SLURM mem-per-cpu in MB for --executor slurm (optional). If set, this overrides --slurm-mem.
  --slurm-mem SLURM_MEM
                        SLURM total memory (--mem) in MB for heavy index rules (default: 409600 ~= 400G). Ignored when --slurm-mem-per-
                        cpu is set.
  --slurm-exclusive     Request exclusive node allocation per submitted SLURM job.
  --organism ORGANISM   Comma-separated subset of organism keys (e.g. Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus)
  --star-index-version STAR_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this STAR index version label.
  --bwa-index-version BWA_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this bwa index version label.
  --bowtie2-index-version BOWTIE2_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this bowtie2 index version label.
  -no-skip-plan, --no-skip-plan
                        Run plan preflight checks before build (config validation, URL checks, lint, dry-run).
```

### Checking for updates

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates --help
```

```text
usage: bcfngs genomes check-updates [-h]

optional arguments:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
```

That means:

- use `--cores`  under `local` executor to control total workflow parallelism
- use `--organism` for a comma-separated subset of organism keys
- use `--star-index-version`, `--bwa-index-version`, and `--bowtie2-index-version` when the resolved tool version should target a matching versioned index explicitly
- using [`snakemake`](updating_genomes.qmd#direct-snakemake-subset-dry-run) directly for advanced cases such as scheduler memory flags or building only one index type is also possible.

## Shared wrapper parameters

These apply to all three genomes subcommands and are cross-referenced from the chapters below.

| Parameter | Current default | Applies to | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--profile` | `micromamba` | `check-updates`, `plan`, `build` | `modules` now resolves `STAR`, `bwa`, and `bowtie2` from modules while keeping `python`, `snakemake`, and `curl` in micromamba. |
| `--env-prefix` | `~/miniconda/env` | `check-updates`, `plan`, `build` | This is the current default in [`scripts/bcfngs`](scripts/bcfngs). Override it if your environment lives elsewhere. |
| `configs/genomes.yaml` | required | `check-updates`, `plan`, `build` | Release pins, organisms, output root, thread defaults, RAM defaults, and index version strings all come from this file. |

## Common config fields

These are the main `configs/genomes.yaml` fields referenced by the three command chapters.

| Config field | Current value | Used by | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `genomes_root` | `/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes` | `plan`, `build` | `plan` checks write access here. `build` writes sources, indices, stable symlinks, and per-organism manifests here. |
| `releases.ensembl` | `115` | `check-updates`, `plan`, `build` | Main Ensembl release pin. `check-updates` compares this to `ftp.ensembl.org`. |
| `releases.ensemblgenomes.metazoa` | `62` | `plan`, `build` | Used for `ensemblgenomes` organisms such as `Drosophila_pseudoobscura`. |
| `default_threads` | `16` | `build` | Used by `STAR` genome generation and `bowtie2-build`. |
| `default_ram_bytes` | `168632718037` | `build` | Passed to `STAR --limitGenomeGenerateRAM`. |
| `index_versions.star` | `""` | `build` | Controls versioned output paths such as `starIndex-<version>`. |
| `index_versions.bwa` | `""` | `build` | Controls versioned output paths such as `bwaIndex-<version>`. |
| `index_versions.bowtie2` | `""` | `build` | Controls versioned output paths such as `bowtie2Index-<version>`. |
| `organisms.*` | all configured organisms | `plan`, `build` | Scientific-name keys such as `Homo_sapiens`, `Mus_musculus`, `Drosophila_melanogaster`. |
| `organisms.*.sa_index` | organism-specific | `build` | Passed to `STAR --genomeSAindexNbases`. |
| `organisms.*.fasta_url` / `gtf_url` | optional explicit URLs | `plan`, `build` | Used for organisms that do not fit the generated Ensembl filename pattern. |

## `check-updates`

### Parameters and defaults

| Item | Current default / value | Where set | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Shared wrapper parameters | see [Shared wrapper parameters](#shared-wrapper-parameters) | CLI | `--profile` and `--env-prefix` still apply here. |
| Command-specific flags | none | CLI | This subcommand has no extra runtime flags beyond `-h/--help`. |
| Compared release pin | `releases.ensembl: 115` | [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) | This is the pinned release compared to the latest Ensembl release found on `https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/`. |
| Network target | `https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/` | internal behavior | If it is unreachable, the command reports the current pin but cannot confirm the latest release. |
| Writes to disk | none | internal behavior | Report only. No files are edited. |
| Not checked automatically | `releases.ensemblgenomes.*`, explicit `fasta_url`, explicit `gtf_url` | config | Review these manually when working with EnsemblGenomes or custom URLs. |

### Micromamba / conda-style environment

Use this when the wrapper should run in its default `micromamba` profile:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates
```

Typical output looks like:

```text
Ensembl latest release: 117  current pinned: 116  status: UPDATE_AVAILABLE
Suggested bump: set releases.ensembl to 117 in configs/genomes.yaml
```

### Modules profile

If you want the same command to run under the `modules` profile:

```bash
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes check-updates
```

For `check-updates`, the modules profile usually does not change the result. It is only relevant if you are keeping your operational workflow consistent across all genomes commands.

### If an update is available

1. Edit [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml).
2. Bump `releases.ensembl`, for example `116` to `117`.
3. Review any organism entries that use explicit `fasta_url` or `gtf_url`.
4. Continue with [`plan`](#plan) and then [`build`](#build).

If you are updating an EnsemblGenomes organism such as `Drosophila_pseudoobscura`, also review `releases.ensemblgenomes` plus the explicit URLs in its organism block.

## `plan`

### Parameters and defaults

| Item | Current default / value | Where set | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Shared wrapper parameters | see [Shared wrapper parameters](#shared-wrapper-parameters) | CLI | `--profile` and `--env-prefix` apply here. |
| Command-specific flags | `--organism`, `--star-index-version`, `--bwa-index-version`, `--bowtie2-index-version` | CLI | Use these to dry-run a subset or validate an explicit tool/index version pairing. |
| Organism scope | all configured organisms | wrapper behavior | The wrapper plans the full configured set. Use direct `snakemake` with `--config org_subset=...` if you want a subset dry-run. |
| `genomes_root` | `/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes` | [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) | `plan` checks that this location is writable. |
| Release pins and URLs | see [Common config fields](#common-config-fields) | config | `plan` resolves all FASTA/GTF URLs from the current release pins and organism blocks, then tests them with `curl -fsSI`. |
| Underlying Snakemake steps | `snakemake --lint` then `snakemake -npr` | wrapper behavior | `plan` is both a lint pass and a dry-run. |

### Local run with micromamba / conda-style environment

Use this before every real build:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
```

This checks that:

- [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) parses
- `genomes_root` is writable
- each configured FASTA/GTF URL responds
- [`workflow/genomes.smk`](workflow/genomes.smk) passes `snakemake --lint`
- the workflow dry-run resolves successfully

### Local run with modules

If your cluster provides aligners via modules:

```bash
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes plan
```

The `modules` profile now resolves mapper binaries explicitly. `python`, `snakemake`, and `curl` stay in micromamba, while `STAR`, `bwa`, and `bowtie2` are reused or loaded from modules. Each top-level command prints the resolved tool/version report to `stderr`.

### Direct Snakemake subset dry-run

The wrapper does not expose a `--organism` option for `plan`. Keep the direct `snakemake` form when you need a subset dry-run:

```bash
snakemake -npr \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  --config org_subset=Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus
```

This is useful for:

- testing a release bump on one organism first
- checking a new organism block before rebuilding everything
- isolating failures in one species without noise from the rest of the config

### SLURM-oriented dry-run example

For clusters where you want to inspect scheduler-facing settings before a real run, keep the direct `snakemake` example:

```bash
snakemake -npr \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --default-resources mem_mb=180000 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml
```

`plan` itself does not submit jobs in dry-run mode, but this is still useful to check that your SLURM executor configuration parses correctly.

## `build`

### Parameters and defaults

| Item | Current default / value | Where set | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Shared wrapper parameters | see [Shared wrapper parameters](#shared-wrapper-parameters) | CLI | `--profile` and `--env-prefix` apply here too. |
| `--cores` | `16` | CLI | Total Snakemake core budget for `./scripts/bcfngs genomes build`. |
| `--organism` | all configured organisms | CLI | Optional comma-separated subset such as `Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus`. |
| `--star-index-version`, `--bwa-index-version`, `--bowtie2-index-version` | resolved binary version | CLI | If set, each flag must match the actual resolved binary version for that tool. |
| `default_threads` | `16` | [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) | Used by `STAR` genome generation and `bowtie2-build`. This is separate from `--cores`. |
| `default_ram_bytes` | `168632718037` | [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) | Passed to `STAR --limitGenomeGenerateRAM`. |
| `index_versions.*` | resolved at runtime | wrapper behavior | Controls versioned output paths such as `starIndex-<version>`. |
| `organisms.*.sa_index` | organism-specific | [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) | Controls STAR suffix-array sizing. |
| Stable symlink outputs | `starIndex`, `bwaIndex`, `bowtie2Index` | wrapper behavior | Repointed after build to the highest built version available for that organism and tool. |
| Per-organism manifest | `index-manifest.json` | wrapper behavior | Written under each organism directory with assembly, source release, tool versions, stable targets, and discovered versioned builds. |
| Advanced scheduler knobs | direct `snakemake` only | CLI at Snakemake level | Use these for SLURM executor settings, `mem_mb`, or `mem_mb_per_cpu`. |

### Local run with micromamba / conda-style environment

Build all configured genomes:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
```

More local examples:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 8
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 32
```

`--cores` is the total Snakemake job budget. It is not the same thing as `default_threads`, which controls per-tool threading inside rules such as `STAR` genome generation.

### Local run with modules

```bash
module load star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes build --cores 20
```

This is the best fit when the aligners should come from modules but `python` and `snakemake` should still come from the micromamba environment.

### Build only selected organisms

Organism names must match the keys in [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml) exactly.

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 12 --organism Drosophila_melanogaster,Drosophila_pseudoobscura
```

Use this when:

- you added or changed only one organism
- you want to test a release bump on a smaller subset first
- you do not want to rebuild every organism after a config edit

### SLURM-oriented build example

We can use the wrapper to run jobs on the lcuster using the `--executor` parameter. This will use the slurm scheduler to control the job management and increase efficiency. The wrapper offers [multiple parameters](updating_genomes.qmd#building-the-genomic-indicies) to control resources when running a slurm job here.

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --executor slurm \
	--organism Caenorhabditis_elegans,Saccharomyces_cerevisiae \
	--slurm-partition PARTITION \
	--slurm-mem 102400 \
```

The command above will execute the index building of two organisms only. It will use a specific partition onour cluster and provide each jonb with 102400 MB of RAM

Looking at the scheduler, when running, one can follows the job execution:

```
             JOBID                                          NAME     USER ST       TIME  NODES NODELIST(REASON)                       COMMENT NODELIST(REASON)
           2457762          2b8db86f-90a2-4ad7-a292-2ef9989aaefd USER      R       0:03      1        hpcl9312rule_bowtie2_index_wildcards_C   #NR_NODE
           2457763          2b8db86f-90a2-4ad7-a292-2ef9989aaefd USER      R       0:03      1        hpcl9312rule_star_index_wildcards_Caen   #NR_NODE
           2457761          2b8db86f-90a2-4ad7-a292-2ef9989aaefd USER      R       0:04      1        hpcl9307rule_star_index_wildcards_Sacc   #NR_NODE
           2457759          2b8db86f-90a2-4ad7-a292-2ef9989aaefd USER      R       0:07      1        hpcl9306rule_bowtie2_index_wildcards_S   #NR_NODE
           2457764          2b8db86f-90a2-4ad7-a292-2ef9989aaefd USER      R       0:03      1        hpcl9320rule_chrom_size_wildcards_Caen   #NR_NODE
           2457765          2b8db86f-90a2-4ad7-a292-2ef9989aaefd USER      R       0:03      1        hpcl9307rule_bwa_index_wildcards_Caeno   #NR_NODE
           2457760          2b8db86f-90a2-4ad7-a292-2ef9989aaefd USER      R       0:04      1        hpcl9306rule_bwa_index_wildcards_Sacch   #NR_NODE
```

Each time the command is executed, the scheduler writes a log file into the `.snakemake/log/` folder. If errors occur, they can be found either in these log files or in the log file for the specific mapper tool under the organism's name, e.g. `genomes/logs/Saccharomyces_cerevisiae/star_index.log`.

### Memory and threading

There are two different layers to tune:

- `--cores`: total workflow parallelism for the outer Snakemake run
- `default_threads`: per-rule thread count used inside `STAR` genome generation and `bowtie2-build`

Typical patterns:

- 20-core workstation, one large STAR job at a time: set `default_threads: 20` and run `./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20`
- 20-core workstation, allow two medium jobs in parallel: set `default_threads: 10` and run `./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20`
- larger-memory node for STAR genome generation: increase `default_ram_bytes`

For a permanent STAR memory change, edit:

```yaml
default_ram_bytes: 250000000000
```

# Build only one index type with direct Snakemake

Replace each version placeholder with the matching value from `index_versions` in [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml).

```bash
snakemake -pr \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --default-resources mem_mb=180000 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml
```

If your site schedules by memory per CPU instead:

```bash
snakemake -pr \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --default-resources mem_mb_per_cpu=8000 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml
```

Adjust these examples to your local cluster conventions. The current `./scripts/bcfngs genomes build` command does not parse `--mem` or `--mem-per-cpu` directly.

Only STAR for `Homo_sapiens`:

```bash
snakemake -pr -j 20 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/Homo_sapiens/starIndex-<STAR_VERSION>/SA
```

Only bwa for `Homo_sapiens`:

```bash
snakemake -pr -j 20 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/Homo_sapiens/bwaIndex-<BWA_VERSION>/Homo_sapiens.bwt
```

Only bowtie2 for `Homo_sapiens`:

```bash
snakemake -pr -j 20 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/Homo_sapiens/bowtie2Index-<BOWTIE2_VERSION>/Homo_sapiens.1.bt2
```

These commands still pull in prerequisite downloads, but they avoid rebuilding every index type.

# Rebuild after a genome release change

After [`check-updates`](#check-updates) reports a newer Ensembl release:

1. Edit [`configs/genomes.yaml`](configs/genomes.yaml).
2. Bump `releases.ensembl`, for example `116` to `117`.
3. Review any explicit `fasta_url` or `gtf_url` entries.
4. Run `plan`.
5. Run `build`.

If you want a cautious rollout, start with one organism:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens
```

## Rebuild after a tool version change

When the genome sequence did not change but an indexer changed, the version label is taken from the tool version installed via conda.

to re-index the genomes with the new version, just rerun:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
```

Successful builds update the stable symlinks automatically to the highest built version detected on disk:

- `/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/starIndex`
- `/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/bwaIndex`
- `/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/bowtie2Index`

These stable names point to versioned directories such as `starIndex-2.7.11b`.

Each organism directory also gets an `index-manifest.json` file recording:

- the assembly and source URLs
- the Ensembl or EnsemblGenomes release used for indexing
- the resolved tool versions used during the last build command
- the current stable symlink targets
- the discovered versioned index directories for STAR, bwa, and bowtie2
